key_insight: An interpretable vision transformer can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina from SEM images, and low-magnification views alone may already carry enough diagnostic information.
engineering_relevance: This could speed up implant failure analysis and quality assurance by enabling low-magnification pre-screening before time-intensive high-magnification fractography.
method: Curated 8,493 SEM fracture images from five years of burst and proof tests.
Fine-tuned a vision transformer to classify three defect origins: green body, hard machining, and material defects.
Used Grad-CAM plus leakage audits to verify interpretability and specimen separation.
evidence_signal: In stratified five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 0.907 accuracy and 0.888 macro-F1, with low-magnification 50x performance comparable to 1k–10kx.
watch_item: The workflow still depends on high-quality labeled fracture datasets and should be validated on new materials, scanners, and production lines before deployment.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
